# builtnoble/vite-php

> A framework-agnostic PHP library that reads Vite's manifest.json and outputs the correct `<script>` and `<link>` tags for both development (HMR) and production environments. Intended as a core utility for integrating Vite into standalone PHP apps or templating engine extensions (Twig, Plates, Laminas View).

## Installation

```bash
composer require builtnoble/vite-php
```

Requires PHP 8.5+.

## Quickstart

```php
use Builtnoble\VitePHP\ViteFactory;

$vite = ViteFactory::make([
    'publicPath' => 'public',   // where your public/ dir is (default: 'public')
    'buildDir'   => 'dist',     // matches vite.config.js build.outDir (default: 'build')
]);

// Call the instance with your entry points — returns an HTML string of <script>/<link> tags
echo ($vite)(['resources/js/app.js', 'resources/css/app.css']);
```

The returned string contains all `<link rel="stylesheet">` tags followed by all `<script type="module">` tags, including any CSS chunks referenced via `imports` in the manifest.

## ViteFactory::make() options

| Option                        | Type                   | Default           | Description |
|-------------------------------|------------------------|-------------------|-------------|
| `publicPath`                  | `string`               | `'public'`        | Path to your public directory |
| `buildDir`                    | `string`               | `'build'`         | Vite output directory (must match `build.outDir` in vite.config.js) |
| `manifestFilename`            | `string`               | `'manifest.json'` | Name of the manifest file inside `{publicPath}/{buildDir}/.vite/` |
| `hotfile`                     | `string`               | `'{publicPath}/hot'` | Path to the HMR hotfile written by the Vite dev server |
| `nonce`                       | `string\|null`         | not set           | CSP nonce added to all tags; pass `null` to auto-generate a 40-char random nonce |
| `integrityKey`                | `string\|false`        | `'integrity'`     | Manifest key for the SRI hash; set `false` to disable |
| `assetPathResolver`           | `callable\|null`       | `null`            | Custom callable `(string $path): string` to rewrite asset URLs |
| `scriptTagAttributesResolvers`| `callable\|callable[]` | `[]`              | Callable(s) `($src, $url, $chunk, $manifest): array` returning extra `<script>` attributes |
| `styleTagAttributesResolvers` | `callable\|callable[]` | `[]`              | Callable(s) `($src, $url, $chunk, $manifest): array` returning extra `<link>` attributes |

A second optional `$creator` callable can be passed to `ViteFactory::make()` to supply a custom `Vite` instance (useful when extending the class).

## Manifest path convention

Manifests are resolved at: `{publicPath}/{buildDir}/.vite/{manifestFilename}`

Example with defaults: `public/build/.vite/manifest.json`

Your `vite.config.js` should set `build.manifest: true` (or `build.manifest: 'manifest.json'`) and `build.outDir: 'public/build'` to match.

## HMR (development) mode

When the hotfile exists on disk, HMR mode is active. The hotfile is written by the Vite dev server and contains the dev server base URL (e.g. `http://localhost:5173`). In this mode, the `@vite/client` script is injected automatically and asset URLs point to the dev server instead of the manifest.

## Getting a single asset URL

```php
$url = $vite->asset('resources/images/logo.png');
```

Works in both HMR and production mode.

## CSP nonce

```php
$vite = ViteFactory::make(['nonce' => null]); // auto-generates a nonce
$nonce = $vite->getNonce();                   // retrieve it to set your CSP header
```

## Custom attribute resolvers

```php
$vite = ViteFactory::make([
    'scriptTagAttributesResolvers' => function (string $src, string $url, ?array $chunk, ?array $manifest): array {
        return ['crossorigin' => 'anonymous'];
    },
    'styleTagAttributesResolvers' => [
        function (string $src, string $url, ?array $chunk, ?array $manifest): array {
            return ['media' => 'all'];
        },
    ],
]);
```

## Custom asset path resolver

Useful when assets are served from a CDN or prefixed path:

```php
$vite = ViteFactory::make([
    'assetPathResolver' => fn (string $path): string => 'https://cdn.example.com/' . $path,
]);
```

## ViteInterface

All public methods are defined in `ViteInterface`. When type-hinting in your own code, use `ViteInterface` rather than the concrete `Vite` class.

## Error handling

`ViteException` is thrown when:
- The manifest file does not exist at the expected path
- The manifest file contains invalid JSON
- An entry point is not found in the manifest
- A creator callable does not return a `ViteInterface` instance

`\Random\RandomException` may be thrown when `nonce => null` is passed and random byte generation fails (rare).
